executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Whole-stack certification remains blocked by the marketing independent fail on primary-repo outcome evidence.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Do not certify green until marketing produces a fresh independent pass backed by measurable Codeberg movement.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks

findings.title: Marketing remains the only outcome-owned red loop
findings.mechanism: Marketing independent verification is still fail and the unresolved blocker remains primary-repo adoption measurement pending.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for Codeberg-primary adoption is still missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification before calling the whole stack green.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology is coherent and freshly localized
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection shows 20 enabled/total jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 4 active runners, and 0 live last-error jobs.
findings.root_cause: Architecture/runtime topology is coherent while marketing outcome evidence remains unresolved.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep live-topology verification tied to direct cron inspection on each watchdog run and avoid treating external blocker clearance as an architecture repair.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is green on freshness and ownership gates
findings.mechanism: Independent verification, loop integrity, docs verification, and runtime market-intelligence consumption remain coherent after the refresh.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining risk is external.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification after each material architecture artifact refresh.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology currently exposes zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Wait for a marketing lane that can move primary-repo adoption and rerun marketing independent verification on the resulting evidence
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing is still the only outcome-owner red domain after the architecture-side runtime checks were refreshed.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining blocker or sharpens the next marketing repair target.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current live inspection is clean and should remain the source of truth instead of persisted-history drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops stale topology claims from reappearing in architecture reports.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed the audit against the current live view: 20 enabled jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 4 active runners, and 0 live last-error jobs.
Revalidated loop integrity, docs independent pass, health-monitor localization, and shared market-intelligence consumption before rerunning independent verification.

independent_verification.summary: Pending rerun after architecture artifact refresh.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh architecture independent verification after this audit refresh.
Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing remains red on Codeberg-primary outcome evidence
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are coherent, but marketing independent verification still fails closed because primary-repo movement is still measurement-pending.

notes: Architecture green here means the architecture-owned verifier path is coherent; it does not mean the whole stack is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are 0.
Independent live inspection in this snapshot saw 20 total jobs, 20 enabled, 0 disabled, 4 running, and 0 live last-error jobs.
The remaining blocker is marketing outcome evidence, not architecture runtime drift.
